## 1. What the Agent Recommended

- **Base:** Jordan Lee — Senior PMM · `PRIVATE__YOUR_FILES_GITIGNORED/00-INTAKE__YOUR_PRIVATE_INFO/01-about-you/resume-jordan-lee.md` (AI Product Marketing family)
- **Summary:** Three options offered. Option 1 = Family A workhorse ("turn complex products into clear positioning…"). Option 2 = anti-hype / grounded-claims angle ("I'm a senior PMM who actually talks to customers…"). Option 3 = hybrid dual-buyer framing (longer). Agent flagged Option 2 as the strongest cultural match for Thornbury's "grounded in real customer outcomes" language.
- **Tilebridge AI bullet:** Replace the base bullet ("Adopted AI assistants…") with the [variant-ai] bullet: "Built an AI-assisted enablement-drafting workflow (I write the brief and edit; the model drafts) that roughly halved first-draft cycle time (directional) — a practitioner's use of AI tools, not ML engineering."
- **Win-loss / roadmap-owner bullet:** Full text recommended: "Run quarterly win-loss and customer-discovery interviews (~40/yr); fed themes into roadmap conversations and the messaging hierarchy (as an input partner, not the roadmap owner)."
- **Birchwood second bullet:** Base included "Mentored one marketing intern and coordinated a freelance designer (no direct reports)."
- **Skills line:** 12 skills recommended, ending with "Pricing & Packaging (Partner)."
- **Positioning bullet qualifier:** "…competitive win rate rose ~9 points (directional, internal)."
- **No changes recommended** to Marlowe or Birchwood (first bullet).

## 2. What Was Submitted

Summary used was a lightly edited version of Option 2. Tilebridge AI bullet was swapped as recommended but the trailing boundary phrase was dropped. The win-loss bullet was trimmed. The Birchwood people-management bullet was removed entirely. Skills dropped one item. One qualifier word was removed from the positioning bullet.

Full submitted text (verbatim from PDF, semantic content):

**Summary:** "I'm a senior PMM who actually talks to customers every quarter — the win-loss and discovery interviews that keep positioning honest. Across ~8 years in B2B SaaS I've built competitive programs from scratch and led GTM for launches across product, sales, and lifecycle. I adopted AI tools early and use them pragmatically in the GTM workflow. Now I want to bring that to an AI-native company, where keeping claims grounded matters most."

**Tilebridge AI bullet (submitted):** "Built an AI-assisted enablement-drafting workflow (I write the brief and edit; the model drafts) that roughly halved first-draft cycle time."

**Win-loss bullet (submitted):** "Run quarterly win-loss and customer-discovery interviews (~40/yr); fed themes into roadmap conversations as an input partner."

**Birchwood:** One bullet only — "Supported launches, wrote product content and case studies, and maintained the early competitive library."

**Skills (submitted, 11 items):** Positioning & Messaging, Product Launches, Go-To-Market Strategy, Competitive Intelligence, Customer & Win-Loss Research, Sales Enablement, Applied AI Tools (Practitioner), Lifecycle & Adoption Marketing, Cross-Functional Leadership, Messaging Hierarchy, Launch Content.

**Positioning bullet qualifier:** "…competitive win rate rose ~9 points (directional)."

## 3. Observed Changes (fact)

**O1 — Summary: Option 2 selected, with two light edits.**
Option 2 was chosen (not Options 1 or 3). Two phrases differ from the agent's verbatim draft:
- Agent: "I adopted AI tools early and pragmatically in the GTM workflow." → Submitted: "I adopted AI tools early and use them pragmatically in the GTM workflow." (added "use them")
- Agent: "Now I want to bring that honesty to an AI-native company" → Submitted: "Now I want to bring that to an AI-native company" (word "honesty" dropped)

**O2 — Tilebridge AI bullet: [variant-ai] swap executed, but trailing clause dropped.**
The swap from the base bullet to [variant-ai] was made as recommended. However, the submitted version omits the tail "— a practitioner's use of AI tools, not ML engineering." The submitted bullet ends at "that roughly halved first-draft cycle time." The "(directional)" qualifier present in the recommendation was also dropped from this bullet.

**O3 — Win-loss bullet: "the messaging hierarchy" and "not the roadmap owner" removed.**
Recommended: "…fed themes into roadmap conversations and the messaging hierarchy (as an input partner, not the roadmap owner)."
Submitted: "…fed themes into roadmap conversations as an input partner."
Two semantic items removed: (a) the phrase "and the messaging hierarchy" (scope of influence); (b) the explicit boundary clause "not the roadmap owner."

**O4 — Birchwood second bullet removed entirely.**
The base file included "Mentored one marketing intern and coordinated a freelance designer (no direct reports)." The agent recommended no changes to Birchwood. The submitted resume carries only the one Birchwood bullet; the people-management/intern bullet is gone.

**O5 — Skills: "Pricing & Packaging (Partner)" omitted.**
Agent recommended 12 skills including "Pricing & Packaging (Partner)" as the final item. Submitted has 11 — that item is absent. All other recommended skills are present and in the recommended order.

**O6 — Positioning bullet qualifier: ", internal" dropped.**
Recommended text: "…competitive win rate rose ~9 points (directional, internal)."
Submitted text: "…competitive win rate rose ~9 points (directional)."
The word "internal" was removed; "(directional)" remains.

## 4. Inferred Lessons (hypotheses + confidence)

**I1 — [O2 / AI bullet tail] Voice preference: the explicit AI-boundary phrase reads as defensive.**
Category: voice | Confidence: med
The [variant-ai] swap was made (agent's core recommendation followed), but the "— a practitioner's use of AI tools, not ML engineering" clause was cut. One plausible reading: Jordan finds the boundary-disclaimer tone defensive or unnecessary when the bullet already signals practitioner use through the workflow mechanic ("I write the brief and edit; the model drafts"). If so, the appropriate inference is that explicit boundary language belongs in the Summary or the skills parenthetical, not appended to a bullet. Cannot confirm without asking.

**I2 — [O3 / win-loss bullet] "not the roadmap owner" removed: boundary framing may feel over-hedged in bullets.**
Category: voice | Confidence: med
The "as an input partner, not the roadmap owner" phrasing was designed to pre-empt scope overclaiming. Jordan kept "as an input partner" but dropped "not the roadmap owner." The inference is that the positive framing ("input partner") is sufficient and the negative qualifier reads as anxious or wordy. If this pattern holds, the lesson is: use the positive framing only; trust the reader not to misread scope. One occurrence; needs confirmation to become a rule.

**I3 — [O3 / win-loss bullet] "the messaging hierarchy" dropped: may be length/density preference, not a claim boundary issue.**
Category: voice | Confidence: low
"Fed themes into roadmap conversations and the messaging hierarchy" was trimmed to "roadmap conversations." The scope of influence (feeding the messaging hierarchy, not just roadmap) is real and approved evidence. It is unclear whether Jordan cut it for space, for simplicity, or because the phrasing felt awkward. Cannot distinguish between an intentional scope reduction and an editorial tightening without asking.

**I4 — [O4 / Birchwood intern bullet] People-management language may create a wrong signal for IC PMM roles.**
Category: content | Confidence: med
The agent recommended no changes to Birchwood and the base bullet survived. The intern/freelance-coordination bullet was removed. A plausible inference: for a Senior IC PMM role (no people-management component in the JD), mentioning "mentored one intern" reads as padding rather than proof, and may dilute the competitive / content focus of the Birchwood block. If so, this is a routing rule: suppress the people-management bullet when the target role is IC-only. One occurrence; flag for confirmation.

**I5 — [O5 / skills] "Pricing & Packaging (Partner)" omitted: may signal a deliberate scope boundary for this role.**
Category: skills | Confidence: low
Thornbury's JD does not mention pricing or packaging. The agent included it as a canonical breadth item. Jordan dropped it. The inference is either (a) Jordan reads skills as strictly JD-mirrored for this application, or (b) he prefers not to flag P&P because it might imply ownership he doesn't have. Cannot tell without asking. Either way, if the lesson is "drop P&P skills for roles where P&P is not in the JD," that is a skills-routing rule worth recording if it recurs.

**I6 — [O1 / summary] "honesty" dropped from the closer: may be a deliberate tone adjustment.**
Category: voice | Confidence: low
"Now I want to bring that honesty to an AI-native company" → "Now I want to bring that to an AI-native company." The word "honesty" was removed. One reading: it felt slightly on-the-nose or presumptuous (claiming the virtue by name). The submitted version lets the antecedent ("the win-loss and discovery interviews that keep positioning honest") do the work without restating it. Low confidence — this is a very minor edit and may be nothing more than feel.

**I7 — [O6 / qualifier] ", internal" dropped: may reflect a preference for minimal parentheticals.**
Category: voice | Confidence: low
The submitted version keeps "(directional)" but drops ", internal." The "(directional)" label already signals uncertainty. Adding ", internal" may have felt redundant (internal is implied by the directional caveat) or excessively cautious. Trivial edit; low signal value alone, but consistent with the broader pattern of trimming defensive clauses (see I1, I2).

---

## 5. Questions for the Candidate

**Q1 — [O2 / AI bullet tail — I1]** The [variant-ai] swap was made but the tail "— a practitioner's use of AI tools, not ML engineering" was dropped. Was this because (a) the workflow mechanic in the bullet already signals practitioner use clearly enough, making the explicit disclaimer feel defensive? Or (b) a space/length decision? This matters: if (a), the boundary phrase should be relocated to the skills parenthetical or summary, not appended to bullets. If (b), no rule change needed.

**Q2 — [O3 / win-loss bullet — I2]** "Not the roadmap owner" was cut from the win-loss bullet. Did you cut it because (a) "as an input partner" is sufficient and the negative qualifier reads as over-hedged, or (b) something else (length, phrasing)? If (a), the lesson is: use the positive framing only in bullets; reserve explicit boundary clauses for places where the risk of misreading is higher.

**Q3 — [O3 / win-loss bullet — I3]** "The messaging hierarchy" was also dropped from that bullet (scope of influence). Was this intentional (you didn't want to claim that scope) or an editorial simplification (the phrase felt wordy)? If intentional, it may affect future approved-truths language around the messaging-hierarchy claim.

**Q4 — [O4 / Birchwood — I4]** The intern/freelance-coordination bullet was removed from Birchwood. Was this because it felt like padding for an IC PMM role, or was there another reason (length, relevance)? If the reason is "IC roles don't need people-management bullets," this becomes a routing rule: suppress that bullet for IC-only target roles.

**Q5 — [O5 / skills — I5]** "Pricing & Packaging (Partner)" was dropped from the skills line. Was this a deliberate scope/relevance decision (not in Thornbury's JD), a preference to keep the skills line shorter, or a boundary concern (not wanting to imply P&P ownership)? This affects whether the skill appears in future applications where P&P is also absent from the JD.

## Base / Template Candidate Assessment

**Is this finalized resume a clean, reusable base for a future role archetype?**

Yes — flag as candidate.

**Archetype:** AI product marketing + workflow-automation SaaS + anti-hype/grounded-claims culture

**Recommendation:** This resume is the first submitted, finalized application from Jordan's pipeline and represents the AI Product Marketing family at its highest-fit configuration. The summary (Option 2, edited), the [variant-ai] bullet, and the skills line as submitted are the tightest combination Jordan has produced for this archetype. Future roles at Series B+ AI-native or AI-tooling companies that emphasize honest positioning, customer research, and pragmatic AI fluency should **copy this resume as the starting point** rather than rebuilding from the base. Promote to the resume index as "Jordan Lee — Sr PMM AI Workflows (Thornbury, 2026)" once Jordan confirms the open questions (Q1–Q5) and is satisfied with the final version.
